destinations.evidence: The outer Pool owns a separate async_pool field alongside, not inside, the locked TxPool.
destinations.snippet:
pool: RwLock<TxPool<T>>,
async_pool: AsyncPool<T::Transaction>,
...
pool: RwLock::new(TxPool::new(ordering, config.clone())),
async_pool: AsyncPool::new(),

destinations.evidence: Canonical insertion, fulfillment, metadata lookup, and fulfilled-entry reads route directly to the dedicated async_pool field.
Sender locking and async metadata storage are owned by AsyncPool and can be consulted independently of TxPool internals.

equivalence_argument: The old AsyncCommitmentPool wording maps to the v2.2 port AsyncPool. The load-bearing behavior is preserved: Pool has a distinct async_pool field, initializes it independently, and routes async sender locks, insertion, lookup, fulfillment, entry enumeration, and cleanup through that field rather than embedding async state in the locked TxPool.
rationale: The diff adds async_pool to Pool, initializes it separately, changes AsyncCommitmentPool::add_transaction to take &self, removes the async subpool from TxPool, and rewires async APIs to call self.async_pool directly. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: This is mostly an internal locking and ownership refactor; callers observe it through async-pool behavior and potential contention/race differences.
statement: The outer Pool must own a dedicated AsyncCommitmentPool field and route async sender-lock checks, metadata lookup, fulfillment, direct result insertion, precompile input lookup, async-pool containment, and async-pool insertion through that field instead of through the locked internal TxPool.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instrument or mock Pool so async result submission, async metadata lookup, and sender-lock checks can occur while the internal TxPool write lock is held, and assert they still complete through the dedicated async_pool; also assert adding a duplicate sender fails via async_pool sender locking.
summary: Async pool state is separate from the internal TxPool lock and owns async routing.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Hold or instrument the internal TxPool lock while calling async metadata lookup, result submission, and sender-lock checks; they should proceed through Pool::async_pool and duplicate sender insertion should fail via AsyncPool sender_locks.
title: Async pool is separate from TxPool lock
